name: Chromatographic Separation
description: Chromatography separates a mixture by differential partitioning between a mobile and a stationary phase: components with stronger stationary-phase affinity are retained longer; band broadening (van Deemter) limits resolution, which is tuned by adjusting phase and flow.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Mixture injected"]
    N2["Carried by mobile phase"]
    N3["Partition w/ stationary phase"]
    N4{"Stronger affinity?"}
    N5["Retained longer"]
    N6["Elutes later"]
    N7["Weak affinity: elutes early"]
    N8["Band broadening"]
    N9{"Peaks resolved?"}
    N10["Adjust phase / flow"]
    N11["Separated components"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|strong| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N4 -->|weak| N7
    N6 --> N8
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|no| N10
    N10 -->|re-run| N2
    N9 -->|yes| N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Longitudinal diffusion and resistance to mass transfer (van Deemter equation)

sources.title: A new form of chromatogram employing two liquid phases (partition chromatography)

sources.title: Principles of Instrumental Analysis, 7th ed. — Introduction to Chromatography
sources.authors: Skoog, D. A.; Holler, F. J.; Crouch, S. R.
